topic: P-wave shadow zone — patch verification (HOL-CROSSREF c3, showTab tab-switch fix)
change_summary: Decider self-applied a one-token onclick fix in WIN-064.detail_evidence: showTab('overview')->showTab('predictions') for the #part6 cross-reference, closing HOL-CROSSREF c3 hole 2 (which the queue reason text mislabels as hole 3; minor docs nit, no impact on the patch).
our_argument_summary: WIN-064 currently argues the P-wave shadow zone at 104-140 deg is decisive evidence FOR a spherical layered Earth (Oldham 1906 / Gutenberg 1913), that the dome's Sub-Terrestrial Wall mechanism is a fixed geographic feature that cannot reproduce the observed azimuthal-symmetric ring around every epicenter worldwide, that the live-power panel does not actually compare prediction (degrees of angular distance) to observation (km of focal depth), and that the non-incompatibility retreat is closed by the dome's explicit 'creates' mechanism wording plus the dome's own 'no exceptions' tolerance-band rule. Verdict: Std Model Explains.

relevant_files: data/wins.json (WIN-064.detail_evidence — diff scope of this verification)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change. The patch is purely a UI-routing onclick fix; it does not touch the science, the citations, the live-panel critique, or the structural tags. Detail_evidence length 3909 -> 3912 (+3 bytes, consistent with 'overview' -> 'predictions' = 8->11 chars).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged from c5 — display panel, not a live validator.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c5. Tolerance: N/A persists in the dome panel; our azimuthal-symmetry attack and non-incompatibility-retreat closure still stand.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Patch does not touch monitor.py or live-power panel evidence.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c5. 104-140 deg = Oldham 1906 / Gutenberg 1913 spherical seismology.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Observation ~120 years old; WIN-064 is new in V51.0.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c5. No raw-text derivation from disc_radius, H(r), or aetheric parameters produces 104-140 deg.

patch_verification.queue_intent: Verify the HOL-CROSSREF c3 patch landed: showTab('overview') -> showTab('predictions') for the Part 6 cross-reference in WIN-064.detail_evidence; confirm the Part 6 link navigates to the Predictions tab.
patch_verification.queue_reason_label_drift: The queue.reason text says "HOL-CROSSREF c3 hole 3" but the patch corresponds to HOL c3 hole 2 (hole 3 is the part2b Registry Framing Audit section-number fix, patched separately under queue_id 413). The text-attribution in HOL c3 hole 2 said "in part4.html" but the actual content was in WIN-064.detail_evidence; the decider correctly identified WIN-064 as the location. No action needed — this is a label drift, not a substantive miss.
patch_verification.diff_scope: wins.json:WIN-064.detail_evidence ONLY — one-token change at the inner onclick of the Part 6 link. No other WIN-064 fields touched. detail_extra unchanged in this commit (its 4310 -> 5317 growth happened in interim commits between c5 on 2026-04-21 and this run; those are out of scope here and would be covered by their own cycle/queue trail).
patch_verification.anchor_validation: #part6 anchor exists once in sections.json:part6.html (verified by grepping id="part6" across all section html bodies). Section part6.tab === "predictions" (confirmed from sections.json). showTab("predictions") + scrollIntoView(#part6) is therefore the correct combination. The pre-patch showTab("overview") would have either errored or dumped the reader on an unrelated tab where #part6 does not resolve.
patch_verification.cross_ref_audit: WIN-064 has exactly two cross-references with anchors: (1) detail_evidence href="#part6" onclick="showTab('predictions')" text="Part 6" — now correct; (2) detail_verdict_text href="#posthoc-standard" with NO showTab. #posthoc-standard lives in section part3 which has tab="wins". WIN-064 itself renders inside the wins tab, so a same-tab anchor link without showTab is correct behavior (browser scrolls within the active tab). No additional showTab patches needed.
patch_verification.side_effects: None observed. Build artifact commit cf116418 followed and is the ordinary auto-build; no out-of-scope content edits riding along.
patch_verification.test_status_at_patch: Build: 3693 tests pass (per decider commit message). Not re-run in this verification, since the patch is a single onclick token with no test surface that wins-content tests would cover separately from build/HTML-link tests, both of which the decider already exercised.

kernel_of_truth.description: Not applicable to a UI-routing patch verification. The kernel-of-truth for the underlying WIN remains as recorded in c5: the shadow zone is genuinely an interesting observational dataset, and the dome's naming of a Sub-Terrestrial Wall mechanism does at least face the explanatory burden head-on rather than punt — but the azimuthal-symmetry geometry refutes the fixed-wall mechanism regardless of naming (see WIN-064.detail_evidence:para2 — the per-epicenter ring requires symmetry-about-every-surface-point, which only a concentric spherical shell satisfies).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Patch scope is UI navigation; it does not change the science. The closing-the-retreat paragraph in WIN-064.detail_extra (production-verb mechanism + non-incompatibility tolerance-band closure) remains the load-bearing argument and is not in this patch's diff.

deeper_analysis:
Verification scope. The HOL-CROSSREF c3 holistic run on 2026-05-29 surfaced four tab/anchor mismatches; the decider self-applied four of them in commit ab3648e3, each pushed as a separate verification queue item (queue_id 412 part1, 413 part2b, 414 WIN-029 — already serviced earlier today — and 415 WIN-064 here). The c3 review noted hole 2's defective text lived in part4 (sections.json:part4.html); the actual matched substring lives in WIN-064.detail_evidence. The decider patched at the real location, leaving sections.json untouched. I verified this by grepping showTab('overview') across all section html bodies — zero matches after the patch — and by confirming WIN-064's only showTab call is now showTab('predictions').

Adversarial check: could a dome defender exploit anything in this patch? No. The patch makes our review more navigationally honest, which is a strict-improvement direction. The defender's realistic complaints about WIN-064 (mechanism specificity, the depth-vs-angle dimensional mismatch, the non-incompatibility retreat) are unrelated to onclick targets and are addressed in detail_evidence prose, not the link routing.

Verify-mode handoff gate (PROP-038): item.class === "verification" ✓, but most-recent review WIN-064.c5 has holes_found.length === 5 (fails gate b) with 2 major holes (fails gate c), so this stays in full-curmudgeon scope rather than handing off to dome-curmudgeon-verify. That is a mechanical-rule outcome; the actual patch verification work is genuinely narrow and could have been verify-mode if c5 holes had been worked down. Surfacing this as a tracker observation in summary_for_decider — the c5 holes (Apr 21) are over a month old now and presumably have ISS records progressing through their own pipeline; once those drain, future minor WIN-064 patches will route to verify-mode automatically.

citations_verified: ab3648e3 commit diff matches queue intent
#posthoc-standard anchor location in sections.json:part3 (same wins tab as WIN-064)
citations_unchecked: c5 unresolved holes from 2026-04-21 — out of scope for this patch verification; assumed tracked under their own ISS records

summary_for_decider: Queue 415 verified. Patch WIN-064.detail_evidence showTab('overview')->showTab('predictions') landed cleanly in ab3648e3 and resolves HOL-CROSSREF c3 hole 2 (the queue reason text mislabels it "hole 3" — minor docs nit, no functional issue). No new holes. Recommendation: close the related ISS without further patching. Two observations for decider hygiene: (1) the queue.reason copy-edit drift ("hole 3" vs actual "hole 2") could be tidied at the source when future HOL-CROSSREF entries are pushed; not blocking. (2) WIN-064.c5 (Apr 21) had 5 holes (2 major, 2 moderate, 1 minor) — over a month old. If those are still open ISSs, they keep this WIN out of verify-mode (PROP-038 gates b+c). Worth a sweep next decider run to see whether any can be closed wontfix-with-rationale, draining the queue for future minor patches.
